item.title: Collaboration Patterns and Socio-Economic Impacts Analysis in Emerging Science and Technology with Machine Learning Algorithms
item.description:
Our study, “Collaboration Patterns and Socio-Economic Impacts Analysis in Emerging Science and Technology with Machine Learning Algorithms,” aims to understand and forecast the societal and economic impacts of emerging Scientific and Technological (S&T) advancements driven by Leading Organizations (LOs) and their Contributing Partners (CPs). By analyzing historical collaboration patterns and using machine learning, we will estimate the effort and investment needed to advance new technologies. Additionally, we will develop interdisciplinary, data-driven solutions to monitor Workforce Development (WFD) and Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, and Accessibility (DEIA) trends within S&T projects. This research received $300,000 in funding from NSF.

Role: Co-PI

Funding Agency: NSF

Program: Division of Innovation and Technology Ecosystems (ITE)

Total Amount: $300K

Period: 09/2024 - 08/2026

item.title: Advancing Hate Speech Detection - A Hybrid Approach Using Large Language Models and Graph Neural Networks
item.description:
Our project, “Advancing Speech Detection: A Hybrid Approach Using Large Language Models and Graph Neural Networks,” receives $400,000 in funding from the NSF. This initiative aims to improve the detection and moderation of hate speech in multilingual contexts to create safer online communities.

Role: PI

Funding Agency: NSF

Program: Computer and Network Systems (CNS)

Total Amount: $400K

Period: 01/2025 - 12/2026

item.title: One project was funded by the National Science Foundation.
item.description: Our project, “Advancing Speech Detection: A Hybrid Approach Using Large Language Models and Graph Neural Networks,” receives $400,000 in funding from the NSF. This initiative aims to improve the detection and moderation of hate speech in multilingual contexts to create safer online communities.

item.description: Our study, “Collaboration Patterns and Socio-Economic Impacts Analysis in Emerging Science and Technology using Machine Learning Algorithms,” aims to understand and forecast the societal and economic impacts of emerging Scientific and Technological (S&T) advancements driven by Leading Organizations (LOs) and their Contributing Partners (CPs). This research received $300,000 in funding from NSF.

item.title: One paper was accepted by ACL.

One paper was accepted by TKDD.
Abhishek Joshi
item.description: Master’s student in Computer Science at Texas A&M University-Corpus Christi.
